user_outcome: An operator's sworn maintainability review revalidates active and retired ownership plus synchronization provenance, preserving disjoint evidence while planning or blocking stale overlap before reuse or dispatch.
rationale: A displayed PASS is not current integration evidence after sibling synchronization. C-08 reconstructs authoritative authored intervals and recognizes only Baton's exact synchronization topology so disjoint sibling bytes survive while overlapping reviewed evidence is invalidated deterministically.
in_scope: Reconstruct each slice's active authoritative interval from its immutable start_commit through the current pinned implementation_head, walking first-parent non-merge authored commits and validating every C-04 semantic path and C-05 report identity.
Retain terminal failed or re-sliced candidate intervals as retired ownership evidence without treating their historical PASS or former implementation_head as current readiness.

Recognize synchronization only as the exact two-parent parent order, unique merge-base, canonical production/shared tree, structural release-wt segment, and release-record composition in planning/normative-clarifications.md section 6; while authority is planning, a historical interval may additionally recognize only an exact sealed entry whose authorization matches the consumer slice, immutable start, exact authoritative review head, start-exclusive/head-inclusive first-parent membership, and freshness_revalidation purpose, after schema, duplicate-key, digest, ordered parent/base, complete exceptional path-set, and every B/P1/P2/result tuple validate. The manifest substitutes only the listed exceptional release-record mismatch set; all ordinary checks still apply to the same merge. Arbitrary merges, wrong-slice/outside-endpoint entries, unlisted or future exceptions, same-path record conflicts, rebases, octopus merges, squash commits, and copied trees are unrecognized.

Compare post-review synchronization contributions to active candidate paths and later authoritative intervals, preserving disjoint additions and, only for a recognized intersecting sync disjoint from later authority, return the exact mutation-free local invalidation plan from section 8 with invalidating merge, parent-2 rollback baseline, invalidated review head, state/result changes, preserved ledger/adjudication, and cleared implementation head.

Fail closed without a transition plan on missing ancestry, rewritten refs, ambiguous ownership, shipped authority, overlap with a later authoritative interval, or any provenance shape that cannot support one deterministic disposition.

Extend the real sworn maintainability review command to revalidate C-08 before report reuse or dispatch; when freshness is valid it exits non-zero with redacted reason readiness_authority_unavailable until S12 lands, while a deterministic invalidation remains a mutation-free plan for S15.
out_of_scope: Computing the canonical semantic manifest and fingerprint belongs to S07-canonical-semantic-scope.
Validating full report blobs and lifecycle phase order belongs to S08-report-ledger-identity and S09-lifecycle-fsm-adjudication.
Executing rollback or deciding overall integration readiness belongs to S12-rollback-readiness.
Performing a merge through a public adapter belongs to S15-unified-track-merge.

effort_complexity.rationale: First-parent history, synchronization topology, active and retired ownership, and post-review overlap interact across multiple immutable Git identities.

risks.description: A merge-only sibling contribution is attributed to a slice or silently invalidates unrelated verified work.
risks.mitigation: Derive authored intervals only from validated first-parent non-merge commits and compare the recognized synchronization contribution path-by-path against pinned C-04 candidate sets.

risks.description: Historical PASS evidence is reused as current readiness after its implementation head has been invalidated.
risks.mitigation: Model retired ownership separately from active authority; retired intervals participate in collision and rollback scope but never satisfy a current PASS predicate.

risks.description: A historical bootstrap manifest entry is treated as global synchronization authority outside the interval that consumed it.
risks.mitigation: Bind recognition to the exact consumer slice, immutable start, exact review head, start-exclusive/head-inclusive first-parent interval, freshness purpose, planning authority, pinned digest and full Git tuple reconstruction; reject wrong-slice, outside-endpoint, unlisted, pre-start, future, recomputed, or caller-selected entries without mutation.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN a linear track contains two slices with valid C-05 authoritative reports and disjoint C-04 candidate sets separated by recognized release-wt synchronization merges THE SYSTEM SHALL reconstruct each slice's first-parent non-merge authored interval, retain both active authorities, and attribute no merge-only sibling path to either slice (C-04, C-05, C-08, N-04, N-06, N-07, N-09).

WHEN a recognized two-parent synchronization merge after the verified frontier changes a path in the newest authoritative review_scope_head candidate set and that set is disjoint from all later authoritative candidates THE SYSTEM SHALL return one mutation-free C-08 transition plan preserving ledger/adjudication, clearing implementation_head, setting maintainability re_slice_required plus overall failed_verification/verification fail, and recording exact invalidating_sync_merge, parent-2 rollback_baseline_commit, and invalidated_review_head for S15 to commit locally without push (C-08, N-06, N-07).

WHILE an earlier slice is terminal re_slice_required and a later slice has a valid authoritative interval, THE SYSTEM SHALL retain the earlier complete candidate set as retired ownership, SHALL NOT count its former PASS as integration authority, and SHALL keep the later disjoint interval active (C-05, C-06, C-08, N-04, N-06).

IF a candidate sync violates any parent, ancestry, structural-segment, canonical non-record/shared tuple, unlisted release-record path, deterministic index-render, or other pathwise rule in planning/normative-clarifications.md section 6—or a sealed entry fails schema, duplicate-key, pinned-digest, planning-authority, consumer-slice, immutable-start, exact-review-head, start-exclusive/head-inclusive interval, freshness_revalidation purpose, merge/parent/base, exact exceptional path-set, or B/P1/P2/result tuple validation, or is wrong-slice, outside-endpoint, unlisted, pre-start, future, recomputed, pattern-matched, or caller-selected—THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL reject it as unrecognized provenance and leave every status and ref unchanged (C-03, C-08, N-06, N-09).

IF a post-sync invalidated candidate set overlaps any later authoritative slice candidate set, provenance is unrecognized/custom, or shipped authority is affected THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL block with both slice IDs and overlapping paths when applicable, require track reconstruction, emit no invalidation transition plan, and leave every lifecycle record and ref unchanged (C-08, N-06, N-07).

WHEN the built sworn binary runs maintainability review over exact C-04/C-05/C-06 evidence with a recognized disjoint synchronization THE SYSTEM SHALL reach C-08 through the public command, preserve reusable authority, then exit non-zero with redacted reason readiness_authority_unavailable and zero dispatch or mutation; recognized stale overlap SHALL return only the deterministic invalidation plan and later overlap, shipped authority, or unrecognized provenance SHALL block without a plan (C-08, N-04, N-06, N-07, N-09).

WHEN an S01 or S02 authoritative interval under planning authority presents its exact sealed consumer slice, immutable start, exact review head, start-exclusive/head-inclusive first-parent membership, and freshness_revalidation purpose, and each listed merge passes every ordinary section-6 check outside its exact exceptional release-record mismatch set THE SYSTEM SHALL recognize those exact entries for freshness reconstruction and preserve or invalidate evidence according to C-08; the same merge for another slice or outside either endpoint SHALL be unrecognized without mutation.
